---
title: "Variable Inclusion: §2.2 Requirements vs. Ellis Implementation"
date: "2026-03-20"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-title: "Sections"
    theme: flatly
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <style>
      table { font-size: 0.85em; }
      td, th { vertical-align: top; }
    </style>
---

This document serves as the traceability record linking the variable requirements specified in
§2.2 of the statistical analysis instructions (`stats_instructions_v3.md`) to their concrete
implementation in the Ellis lane (`manipulation/2-ellis.R`). One table is provided for each
row of the §2.2 requirements table. Each table is preceded by the verbatim passage from §2.2
that defines that variable category.

**Column definitions:**

- **Requested** — Variable as described in §2.2 (conceptual name or derived label).
- **2011** — Actual column name in the CCHS 2010–2011 PUMF after `janitor::clean_names()`.
- **2014** — Actual column name in the CCHS 2013–2014 PUMF after `janitor::clean_names()`.
- **In Study** — Column name in the analytical dataset produced by Ellis (`cchs_analytical.parquet`). Continuous raw variables retain their PUMF name; factor-recoded variables receive a new semantic name.
- **Note** — Implementation status and any discrepancy between white-list and actual PUMF name.

**Status symbols used in the Note column:**

- ✅ Delivered as-is — white-list name matches PUMF exactly; variable present and populated in both cycles.
- ⚠️ Alias required — PUMF uses a different name than the white-list entry; alias map or whitelist fix needed. Column exists in current output but is all-NA until the fix is applied.
- ❌ Absent from PUMF — variable suppressed in the Public Use Microdata File; not obtainable without RDC access.

---

## 1. Outcome — Total Days Absent (All Health Reasons)

> *Days absent from work (past 3 months) – all health reasons combined (count) |
> LOP module – sum across all reason categories*

The primary outcome `days_absent_total` is constructed by Ellis as the row-wise sum of eight
Loss of Productivity (LOP) component variables. When all eight are `NA` for a respondent,
the outcome is set to `0` (structural zero — no reported absence). Values outside the
0–90 day range are set to `NA` with a warning. Both PUMF cycles use identical variable
names for all eight components.

| Requested | 2011 | 2014 | In Study | Note |
|-----------|------|------|----------|------|
| Days absent — chronic condition (component) | `lopg040` | `lopg040` | `lopg040` | ✅ Also the sensitivity outcome (§2 row 2) |
| Days absent — injury (component) | `lopg070` | `lopg070` | `lopg070` | ✅ |
| Days absent — cold (component) | `lopg082` | `lopg082` | `lopg082` | ✅ |
| Days absent — flu / influenza (component) | `lopg083` | `lopg083` | `lopg083` | ✅ |
| Days absent — stomach flu / gastroenteritis (component) | `lopg084` | `lopg084` | `lopg084` | ✅ |
| Days absent — respiratory infection (component) | `lopg085` | `lopg085` | `lopg085` | ✅ |
| Days absent — other infectious disease (component) | `lopg086` | `lopg086` | `lopg086` | ✅ |
| Days absent — other physical / mental health reason (component) | `lopg100` | `lopg100` | `lopg100` | ✅ |
| **Derived outcome** | — | — | `days_absent_total` | ✅ Ellis-computed row-wise sum of the 8 LOP components above |

---

## 2. Outcome (Sensitivity) — Days Absent Due to Chronic Conditions Only

> *Days absent from work due to chronic conditions only |
> LOP module – chronic condition reason only*

The sensitivity outcome `days_absent_chronic` is taken directly from the single LOP component
that records days lost specifically because of the respondent's own chronic condition.

| Requested | 2011 | 2014 | In Study | Note |
|-----------|------|------|----------|------|
| Days absent — chronic condition reason only | `lopg040` | `lopg040` | `days_absent_chronic` | ✅ Single-component copy of `lopg040`; stored separately for sensitivity analysis |

---

## 3. Primary Exposure — 19 Chronic Conditions

> *19 chronic conditions (binary each): arthritis, asthma, cardiovascular disease/stroke,
> hypertension, fibromyalgia, diabetes, migraine, back problems, cancer, COPD, mental illness
> (depression/bipolar/etc.), bowel disorders, digestive diseases, chronic fatigue syndrome,
> multiple chemical sensitivities, mood disorder, anxiety disorder, intestinal/stomach ulcer
> [check exact groupings in Appendix 3 of the thesis] |
> CCC module*

All CCC variables are recoded to binary factors (`"Yes"` / `"No"`); numeric response codes
6, 7, 8, 9, and 96–99 are set to `NA`. Two conditions —
`ccc_300` (other mental illness) and `ccc_185` (digestive disease) — are suppressed in the
PUMF and cannot be recovered without RDC access. Note that cardiovascular disease and stroke
are represented as two separate CCC variables in the PUMF.

| Requested condition | 2011 | 2014 | In Study | Note |
|--------------------|------|------|----------|------|
| Asthma | `ccc_031` | `ccc_031` | `cc_asthma` | ✅ factor: Yes / No |
| Fibromyalgia | `ccc_041` | `ccc_041` | `cc_fibromyalgia` | ✅ factor: Yes / No |
| Arthritis (excl. fibromyalgia) | `ccc_051` | `ccc_051` | `cc_arthritis` | ✅ factor: Yes / No |
| Back problems (excl. fibromyalgia/arthritis) | `ccc_061` | `ccc_061` | `cc_back_problems` | ✅ |
| Hypertension | `ccc_071` | `ccc_071` | `cc_hypertension` | ✅ |
| Migraine | `ccc_081` | `ccc_081` | `cc_migraine` | ✅ |
| COPD / chronic bronchitis / emphysema | `ccc_091` | `ccc_091` | `cc_copd` | ✅ |
| Diabetes | `ccc_101` | `ccc_101` | `cc_diabetes` | ✅ |
| Cardiovascular disease / heart disease | `ccc_121` | `ccc_121` | `cc_heart_disease` | ✅ |
| Cancer (any type) | `ccc_131` | `ccc_131` | `cc_cancer` | ✅ |
| Intestinal / stomach ulcer | `ccc_141` | `ccc_141` | `cc_ulcer` | ✅ |
| Stroke (cardiovascular) | `ccc_151` | `ccc_151` | `cc_stroke` | ✅ |
| Bowel disorder (Crohn's / colitis / IBS) | `ccc_171` | `ccc_171` | `cc_bowel_disorder` | ✅ |
| Chronic fatigue syndrome (CFS) | `ccc_251` | `ccc_251` | `cc_chronic_fatigue` | ✅ |
| Multiple chemical sensitivities (MCS) | `ccc_261` | `ccc_261` | `cc_chemical_sensitiv` | ✅ |
| Mood disorder (depression / bipolar / mania / dysthymia) | `ccc_280` | `ccc_280` | `cc_mood_disorder` | ✅ |
| Anxiety disorder (phobia / OCD / panic disorder) | `ccc_290` | `ccc_290` | `cc_anxiety_disorder` | ✅ |
| Other mental illness (schizophrenia, etc.) | ❌ absent | ❌ absent | ❌ absent | ❌ `ccc_300` not present in either PUMF cycle; suppressed for confidentiality |
| Digestive disease (other than ulcer / bowel disorder) | ❌ absent | ❌ absent | ❌ absent | ❌ `ccc_185` not present in either PUMF cycle; suppressed for confidentiality |

**Coverage summary:** 17 of 19 conditions are available in the PUMF and present in the
dataset as binary factor columns. Two conditions (`ccc_300`, `ccc_185`) require RDC access
or a re-grouping strategy (Appendix 3 of the thesis may specify acceptable substitutes).

---

## 4. Predisposing Variables

> *Age (continuous, recoded in 3 categories: 15–24, 25–54, 55–75); sex; marital status;
> household size; number of children (< 5 yrs, 6–11 yrs, ≥ 12 yrs); education; immigration
> status; ethnic origin (White vs. visible minority); homeownership; student status |
> Main questionnaire*

Age is available in PUMF only as a grouped derived variable (`dhhgage`); single-year
resolution requires restricted RDC access. Several household-level derived variables
(`dhhdfc5`, `dhhdfc11`, `dhhdfc12p`) use names that do not match the actual PUMF columns
(`dhhgle5`, `dhhg611`). Student status (`sdcdgstud`) is suppressed in the PUMF.

| Requested | 2011 | 2014 | In Study | Note |
|-----------|------|------|----------|------|
| Age (grouped; recoded to 15–24, 25–54, 55–75) | `dhhgage` | `dhhgage` | `age_group` | ✅ Ordered factor: "15-24" / "25-54" / "55-75"; raw `dhhgage` also retained |
| Sex | `dhh_sex` | `dhh_sex` | `sex` | ✅ Factor: Male / Female |
| Marital status | `dhhgms` | `dhhgms` | `marital_status` | ✅ Factor: Single / Married / Common-law / Widowed/Divorced/Separated |
| Household size (number of persons) | `dhhghsz` | `dhhghsz` | `dhhdghsz` | ✅ Alias `dhhdghsz ← dhhghsz` resolves; numeric column present and populated |
| Children < 5 yrs in household (count) | `dhhgle5` | `dhhgle5` | ❌ absent | ❌ White-list used `dhhdfc5` (wrong name); correct PUMF name is `dhhgle5`; requires whitelist fix |
| Children 6–11 yrs in household (count) | `dhhg611` | `dhhg611` | ❌ absent | ❌ White-list used `dhhdfc11` (wrong name); correct PUMF name is `dhhg611`; requires whitelist fix |
| Children ≥ 12 yrs in household (count) | unknown | unknown | ❌ absent | ❌ White-list used `dhhdfc12p`; no PUMF equivalent found in either cycle |
| Education level (4 categories) | `edudh04` | `edudh04` | `education` | ✅ Ordered factor: Less than secondary → Post-secondary graduate; `edudr04` (alternate) also present. ⚠️ Cross-cycle label discrepancy for code 3 ("Other post-secondary" in 2010 vs "Some post-secondary" in 2014; see `cchs_value_label_diffs.csv`) |
| Immigration status | `sdcfimm` | `sdcfimm` | `immigration_status` | ✅ Factor: Non-immigrant / Immigrant / Non-permanent resident |
| Ethnic origin (White vs. visible minority) | `sdcgcgt` | `sdcgcgt` | `visible_minority` | ✅ Alias `sdcdgcb ← sdcgcgt` resolves; Factor: White / Visible minority |
| Homeownership / living arrangement | `dhhglvg` | `dhhglvg` | `homeownership` (all NA) | ⚠️ White-list used `dhhdglvg` (extra D); correct PUMF name is `dhhglvg` (8-cat living arrangement, not binary homeownership); column present but all-NA |
| Student status | ❌ absent | ❌ absent | `student_status` (all NA) | ❌ `sdcdgstud` not present in PUMF; column created by `ensure_columns()` but all-NA |

---

## 5. Facilitating Variables

> *Household income (5 categories); province/territory of residence (11 categories);
> having a family doctor; employment type (self-employed vs. employee); work schedule
> (full-time vs. part-time); occupation category; job stress level; fruit/vegetable
> consumption; alcohol use; smoking status; BMI/obesity category; physical activity level |
> Main questionnaire*
file:///C:/Users/andriy.koval/Documents/GitHub/absent-from-chronic/analysis/data-primer-1/univariate-distributions.html
Most facilitating variables are present in both cycles under identical PUMF names or resolve
via the alias map. Five source variables are absent from the PUMF (`alcdgtyp`, `hwtdgbmi`,
`noc_31`, and their factor output columns are all-NA). Three alias-resolved variables
(`incdghh`, `lbfdghp`, `lbfdgft`) are populated.

| Requested | 2011 | 2014 | In Study | Note |
|-----------|------|------|----------|------|
| Household income — derived 5-category | `incghh` | `incghh` | `income_5cat` | ✅ Alias `incdghh ← incghh` resolves; Ordered factor: < $20k → $80k+ |
| Province / territory of residence | `geogprv` | `geogprv` | `geodgprv` | ✅ Alias `geodgprv ← geogprv` resolves; retained as integer |
| Having a regular family doctor | `hcu_1aa` | `hcu_1aa` | `has_family_doctor` | ✅ Factor: Yes / No |
| Employment type (self-employed vs. employee) | `lbsg31` | `lbsg31` | `employment_type` | ✅ Alias `lbfdghp ← lbsg31` resolves; Factor: Employee / Self-employed / Unpaid family worker |
| Work schedule (full-time vs. part-time) | `lbsdpft` | `lbsdpft` | `work_schedule` | ✅ Alias `lbfdgft ← lbsdpft` resolves; Factor: Full-time / Part-time |
| Occupation category (NOC major group) | ❌ absent | ❌ absent | `occupation_category` (all NA) | ❌ `noc_31` not present in PUMF; correct variable is `lbsgsoc` (5-cat); column created by `ensure_columns()` but all-NA |
| Job stress level | `gen_07` | `gen_07` | `job_stress` | ✅ Ordered factor: Not at all stressful → Extremely stressful. Note: GEN_07 is perceived LIFE stress; GEN_09 is perceived WORK stress — confirm intended variable |
| Total daily fruit & vegetable consumption | `fvcgtot` | `fvcgtot` | `fvcdgtot` | ✅ Alias `fvcdgtot ← fvcgtot` resolves; numeric (3-cat derived: <5, 5-10, >10/day) |
| Alcohol use (type of drinker — derived) | `alcdttm` | `alcdttm` | `alcohol_type` (all NA) | ⚠️ White-list used `alcdgtyp` (wrong name); correct PUMF name is `alcdttm`; column all-NA until whitelist fix |
| Smoking status (derived) | `smkdsty` | `smkdsty` | `smoking_status` | ✅ Ordered factor: Never / Former / Occasional / Daily |
| BMI category (derived) | `hwtgisw` | `hwtgisw` | `bmi_category` (all NA) | ⚠️ White-list used `hwtdgbmi` (wrong name); correct PUMF name is `hwtgisw`; column all-NA until whitelist fix |
| Physical activity index (derived) | `pacdpai` | `pacdpai` | `physical_activity` | ✅ Ordered factor: Active / Moderately active / Inactive |

---

## 6. Needs Variables

> *Functional limitations (activity limitations); injury status;
> self-perceived general health; self-perceived mental health;
> self-perceived health compared to prior year |
> Main questionnaire*

All five needs variables are present in both cycles. Self-perceived mental health is
captured by `gen_02b` (alias from `gen_02a` in the white-list). Health compared to
prior year is `gen_02` (not to be confused with mental health).

| Requested | 2011 | 2014 | In Study | Note |
|-----------|------|------|----------|------|
| Functional / activity limitations | `rac_1` | `rac_1` | `activity_limitation` | ✅ Factor: Yes / No (RAC_1 is 3-cat: 1=Sometimes, 2=Often, 3=Never; codes 1+2 → Yes, 3 → No) |
| Injury status (past 12 months) | `inj_01` | `inj_01` | `injury_past_year` | ✅ Factor: Yes / No |
| Self-perceived general health | `gen_01` | `gen_01` | `self_health_general` | ✅ Ordered factor: Excellent → Poor |
| Self-perceived mental health | `gen_02b` | `gen_02b` | `self_health_mental` | ✅ White-list entry `gen_02a` resolved by alias map to `gen_02b`; ordered factor: Excellent → Poor |
| Self-perceived health compared to 1 year ago | `gen_02` | `gen_02` | `health_vs_lastyear` | ✅ Ordered factor: Much better → Much worse; GEN_02 = health vs. 1 yr ago (not mental health) |

---

## 7. Survey Design Variables

> *Survey weights (master weight or annual weight); bootstrap weights (500 replicates per
> cycle) for variance estimation; strata and cluster identifiers |
> CCHS sampling design files*

The master survey weight is present in both cycles. Per Statistics Canada guidelines for
pooling two cycles, `wts_m` is halved (`wts_m / 2`) in Ellis before analysis. Bootstrap
weights are distributed by Statistics Canada as a **separate supplemental file** and are
not bundled with the standard PUMF download; they are absent from both source `.sav` files
in this project. The cluster identifier is suppressed in the PUMF. The health region proxy
`geodpmf` serves as the strata variable but does not capture the full complex sampling design.

| Requested | 2011 | 2014 | In Study | Note |
|-----------|------|------|----------|------|
| Master / annual survey weight | `wts_m` | `wts_m` | `wts_m_pooled` | ✅ Ellis divides by 2 (`wts_m / 2`) when pooling; original preserved as `wts_m_original` |
| Bootstrap weights (500 replicates) | ❌ absent | ❌ absent | ❌ absent | ❌ **Critical blocker.** `bsw001`–`bsw500` are in a separate Statistics Canada supplement file; absent from both PUMF `.sav` files; §5.3–5.10 bootstrap CIs cannot be computed without them |
| Strata identifier | `geodpmf` | `geodpmf` | `geodpmf` | ✅ Stored as character; health-region proxy; does not fully replicate the CCHS stratification frame |
| Cluster identifier | ❌ absent | ❌ absent | ❌ absent | ❌ Suppressed in PUMF for respondent confidentiality; full PSU identifiers require RDC access |

---

## Summary of Gaps

The following variables requested in §2.2 are not obtainable from the public PUMF files and
require either (a) restricted RDC access to the CCHS master file, or (b) alternative coding
strategies:

| Variable | Reason absent | Impact |
|----------|--------------|--------|
| `ccc_300` — other mental illness | Suppressed in PUMF | 1 of 19 chronic conditions missing |
| `ccc_185` — digestive disease | Suppressed in PUMF | 1 of 19 chronic conditions missing |
| `sdcdgstud` — student status | Suppressed in PUMF | 1 predisposing predictor missing (all-NA) |
| `noc_31` — occupation category (NOC) | Suppressed in PUMF; correct variable is `lbsgsoc` (5-cat) | 1 facilitating predictor missing (all-NA); fixing whitelist to `lbsgsoc` would recover it |
| `alcdgtyp` — alcohol type | Wrong white-list name; correct PUMF name is `alcdttm` | 1 facilitating predictor all-NA; fixable |
| `hwtdgbmi` — BMI category | Wrong white-list name; correct PUMF name is `hwtgisw` | 1 facilitating predictor all-NA; fixable |
| `dhhdglvg` — homeownership/living arrangement | Wrong white-list name; correct PUMF name is `dhhglvg` | 1 predisposing predictor all-NA; fixable but semantics change (8-cat living arrangement, not binary homeownership) |
| `dhhdfc5` — children < 5 yrs | Wrong white-list name; correct PUMF name is `dhhgle5` | 1 predisposing predictor absent; fixable |
| `dhhdfc11` — children 6–11 yrs | Wrong white-list name; correct PUMF name is `dhhg611` | 1 predisposing predictor absent; fixable |
| `dhhdfc12p` — children ≥ 12 yrs | No PUMF equivalent found | 1 predisposing predictor absent; may need derivation |
| Bootstrap weights (`bsw001`–`bsw500`) | Separate supplemental file; not in PUMF `.sav` | **Critical**: §5.3–5.10 bootstrap CIs cannot be computed without this file |
| Cluster / PSU identifier | Suppressed in PUMF | Full complex-sample variance estimation requires master file |

**Summary**: Of the 10 missing INFERRED variables, **6 are fixable** by correcting the white-list
names in `manipulation/2-ellis.R`. The remaining 4 (`ccc_300`, `ccc_185`, `sdcdgstud`,
`dhhdfc12p`) are genuinely absent from the PUMF.
